The transition is wired today — the finding's hedge ("if this were ever wired") understates the actual state.

gamemd side verified by SHELL_TRANSITION_ON_MAIN_MENU_CLICK_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §2-3: exhaustive byte-pattern search across all 8 register encodings for the +0xC1 setter confirms exactly one writer (FUN_00608380 at 0x006083D3), reachable only from CDFileClass__Constructor save-success branch. The main-menu dialog proc (0x00531F60), launcher (0x00531CC0), and cleanup (0x00622720) were each decompiled and confirmed to never set +0xC1 on dialog 0xE2. FUN_00608070's slide-out gate checks +0xC1 first and bails immediately for dialog 0xE2 (§3 step 7). DestroyWindow runs instantly, no animation, no input-blocking window. The research doc is HIGH confidence on this negative finding, backed by live Ghidra decompilation calls for all load-bearing functions plus byte-pattern exhaustive search.

The drift is concrete: every main-menu→Skirmish click produces a 420 ms window where Rust accepts zero input events; gamemd has no such window on this path. The module's own //! header already labels itself "bridge/DRIFT code."

Correction to finding framing: the transition IS already wired (not hypothetically so); the 420 ms dead window fires in the current codebase on every main-menu Skirmish click, not only "if this were ever wired."

findings.title: No ShellButtonSlideSound is played during the transition — correct, since stock key is empty
findings.gamemd_behavior: Stock rules.ini/rulesmd.ini have ShellButtonSlideSound= (empty), so even where the native slide runs, no slide sound is audible; the click cue is GUIMainButtonSound/GenericClick=MenuClick from the owner-draw button path. The Rust transition correctly emits no slide sound.
findings.rust_behavior: The transition compositor plays no audio, matching the empty stock key. Flagged unchecked only to note the button-click MenuClick cue belongs to the button press path, not this transition module, and was not audited here.
findings.evidence: ini/rules.ini:586 / ini/rulesmd.ini:712 (ShellButtonSlideSound= empty); SHELL_MENU_TRANSITION_SYSTEM_MODEL_SYNTHESIS.md §3 (stock silent)

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Checked via Read of src/app_shell_transition.rs lines 117-181 (the full render path) and the INI files at rules.ini:586 and rulesmd.ini:712. The Rust transition module plays no audio — confirmed by inspection: lines 117-181 contain only render/compositor calls with zero audio emission. The module's own doc comment (line 3) labels it "bridge/DRIFT code," so there is no claim of native parity for the transition mechanism itself.

The gamemd side is verified via SHELL_BUTTON_SLIDE_SOUND_CALL_SITE_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (2026-05-18, all findings High confidence). Key facts: (1) The single live consumer of RulesClass+0x750 (ShellButtonSlideSound) is at 0x00607F59 inside FUN_006071E0, triggered only on DL=1 (show/open direction). (2) Dialog 0xE2 (the initial main menu) calls FUN_006071E0 only with XOR DL,DL (verified at 0x00622CA6 in FUN_00622B50), so it never reaches the sound call. (3) The stock empty INI means VocClass__PlayAtPos receives a -1 sentinel and produces no audio even if the call site were reached. (4) The sound fires on the Load Game success path — not the main-menu-to-skirmish transition.

The finding's net output claim — no slide sound plays during the main-menu shell transition — is correct. The Rust module plays no audio; gamemd's dialog-0xE2 path plays no audio (DL=0 short-circuits past the sound). Both produce silent output for this specific transition. The INI-empty fallback is a secondary reason (accurate) but the structural reason is the DL=0 path taken by the initial menu.

The finding slightly mis-states the gamemd mechanism by saying "even where the native slide runs, no slide sound is audible" — the more precise statement is that the native slide for dialog 0xE2 always runs in close-direction (DL=0), which structurally bypasses the sound call, and additionally the empty INI would suppress it even if DL were 1. The correction is minor and does not change the net verdict. The finding's core claim (Rust silence matches gamemd silence for this transition) is proven by the binary evidence.
verdict_obj.correction: The finding says "even where the native slide runs, no slide sound is audible" — this should say: for dialog 0xE2 (initial main menu), the native slide always runs in close-direction (DL=0), which structurally bypasses the ShellButtonSlideSound call at 0x00607F59 before the empty-INI fallback even applies. The empty stock INI is a secondary silence reason. ShellButtonSlideSound IS audible in YR on the Load Game success path (FUN_00608260 -> FUN_006071E0 with DL=1), just not for the main-menu dialog specifically.

findings.title: Destination surface composited is a fully-rendered Skirmish shell render target; gamemd's slide composites SHP transition frames (SDBTNANM/SDMPBTN/SDWRNTMP) on the existing surface
findings.rust_location: src/app_shell_transition.rs:141-159 (renders main-menu shell into source RT, Skirmish shell into destination RT, then composites)
findings.gamemd_behavior: FUN_006071E0 redraws SHP transition-frame shapes (SDBTNANM frames, plus optional SDMPBTN/SDWRNTMP groups behind dialog-record flags, and SDTP/radar) directly onto the display surfaces DAT_00887308/DAT_00887310; it does not pre-render two complete dialogs and blend them.
findings.rust_behavior: Builds two complete offscreen color targets (full main-menu shell and full Skirmish shell) and slides/blends them. The asset composition (which SHPs, which frames, flag-gated groups) is entirely bypassed.
findings.evidence: SHELL_TRANSITION_CALLER_00612690_OWNER_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §8 (transition SHPs inside FUN_006071E0, display surfaces DAT_00887308/10) and §9 row 4 ('Rust whole-screen bridge composites source/destination targets, not child-scheduled records'); app_shell_transition.rs:141-159

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed at `src/app_shell_transition.rs:141-159`: the code calls `render_main_menu_shell_to_target` into `pass.source_render_target()` and `render_skirmish_shell_to_target` into `pass.destination_render_target()`, then composites them via `pass.draw(..., progress)`. The module `//!` header (lines 4-5) explicitly self-labels this "bridge/DRIFT code." No refutation is possible from the Rust side.

gamemd side confirmed via two Ghidra-verified research docs. `SKIRMISH_FUN_006071E0_SHELL_TRANSITION_REDRAW_PATH_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` §3.2 (verified via `decompile_function 0x006071E0`) shows `FUN_006071E0` snapshots surface rectangles, enumerates child controls via `FUN_0060A180`/`FUN_0060A250`, builds a per-control timing array, loops for `max(schedule)+6` frames, calls `CC_Draw_Shape` with flags `0x400` and arg `1000` per child, flushes/unlocks display surfaces `DAT_00887308/10`, then sleeps `0x1E` ms per frame. It does not pre-render two full dialogs. Asset groups `SDBTNANM`/`SDMPBTN`/`SDWRNTMP` are per-control schedule entries; optional flag-gated groups `+0xD5/D6/D7` are verified reads at `0x0060727D`, `0x00607294`, `0x006076DE`.

`SHELL_TRANSITION_CALLER_00612690_OWNER_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` §9 row 4 states verbatim: "Rust whole-screen bridge composites source/destination targets, not child-scheduled records." This is the doc cited in the finding's evidence field and it exactly matches.

No refutation basis: the two approaches produce structurally different outputs — different SHP assets, different per-child frame indices, different flag-gated group membership, different end-broadcast messages (`0x4ED` vs `0x4EC` / text-reveal trigger) — so algebraic equivalence is impossible. The "rare" trigger-frequency label is accurate (every session entry fires this once).

findings.title: Composite uses a Linear-filtering sampler, resampling pixel-art shell surfaces during the slide
findings.gamemd_behavior: gamemd blits/draws shell chrome 1:1 with CC_Draw_Shape (nearest, integer pixel positions); there is no bilinear resampling of the shell during any transition.
findings.rust_behavior: The composite sampler is Linear, and the source/destination offsets, while round()-ed to whole pixels, sample through a linear filter that softens edges if any sub-pixel UV arises from the slide; pixel-art crispness is not guaranteed to match a nearest blit.
findings.evidence: shell_transition_pass.rs:58-59 (mag_filter/min_filter Linear); SHELL_DIALOG_SLIDE_TRANSITION_TRANSFORM_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §3 (CC_Draw_Shape frame draws)

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed: `shell_transition_pass.rs:58-59` sets `mag_filter` and `min_filter` to `FilterMode::Linear`; `shell_transition.wgsl:39` calls `textureSample` through that sampler with pixel-shifted UVs (offsets up to ±64px in `fs_main`). This is real.

gamemd side refuted: The finding states gamemd "blits/draws shell chrome 1:1 with CC_Draw_Shape (nearest, integer pixel positions); there is no bilinear resampling of the shell during any transition." This mis-states what gamemd actually does. `SHELL_DIALOG_SLIDE_TRANSITION_TRANSFORM_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §3` (the doc the finding itself cites, verified via `FUN_006071E0` full decompile at `0x006071E0..0x00607FC0`) establishes with HIGH confidence: **there is no per-frame x/y translation in the body at all**. No `SetWindowPos` inside the loop, no per-frame rect mutation. The visual effect is SHP frame index cycling across Win32 child controls — cells step through pre-rendered frames 10→5 (show) or 5→10 (close) with a 1-frame stagger, producing an apparent wave. It is not a positional slide.

The Linear-vs-Nearest comparison has no gamemd referent: there is no "CC_Draw_Shape blit during a positional slide" to compare against. The finding constructs a false parity comparison between Rust's GPU texture compositor (which offsets textures by ±64px) and a gamemd mechanism that does not move pixels at all.

Additionally, `shell_transition_pass.rs:1-5` (the `//!` header) explicitly labels this pass "not a verified native shell transition" — it is an acknowledged placeholder, not a port of the gamemd mechanism. The real drift between Rust and gamemd is the entire architectural approach (GPU texture slide compositor vs SHP-frame cycling on Win32 controls), not the sampler filter mode within the compositor.
verdict_obj.correction: The finding's gamemd claim is wrong. gamemd does not perform a CC_Draw_Shape positional slide during the transition. The binary shows the "slide-in" is SHP frame index animation on Win32 child controls with no per-frame x/y offset (SHELL_DIALOG_SLIDE_TRANSITION_TRANSFORM_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §3, HIGH confidence). There is no nearest-blit positional slide to compare the Linear sampler against. The actual drift is that the Rust implementation uses a GPU texture-slide compositor (with positional offsets) where gamemd uses Win32 SHP-frame cycling — an architecture mismatch acknowledged in the Rust module's own header comment.

findings.title: Parent shell background MNSCRNL.SHP / MNSCRNS.SHP is never composited
findings.rust_location: src/render/main_menu_shell_chrome.rs:48-109 (build_main_menu_shell_chrome_atlas loads only SDTP/SDBTNBKGD/SDBTM/LWSCRNS/L/SDBTNANM); src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:210-241 (build_chrome_instances draws no parent background)

This fills the whole screen client behind the movie panel and right-panel stack.

MNSCRNL/MNSCRNS are not loaded at all, so every screen region not covered by the 632x570 movie or the 168px right panel is left at CLEAR_COLOR instead of the retail background art.
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md 'Parent Background and Right Panel Assets' (record[0x39]=MNSCRNS.SHP, record[0x3A]=MNSCRNL.SHP via SHELL.PAL); SHELL_PARENT_BSURFACE_COMPOSITION_AND_FLIP_GHIDRA_REPORT.md step 3 'Background_Overlay(...) draws MNSCRNS/MNSCRNL into BSurface'

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side verified: `build_main_menu_shell_chrome_atlas` (src/render/main_menu_shell_chrome.rs lines 48-109) loads only SDTP, SDBTM, SDBTNBKGD, LWSCRNS, LWSCRNL, and SDBTNANM — MNSCRNS.SHP and MNSCRNL.SHP are absent. `build_chrome_instances` (src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs lines 210-241) draws only those loaded assets; no parent background sprite is emitted. gamemd side verified via `SHELL_PARENT_BSURFACE_COMPOSITION_AND_FLIP_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` §2.Q4 (step 3 in the per-WM_PAINT sequence: "Background_Overlay(convert, small_shp, large_shp) — draws MNSCRNS/MNSCRNL into BSurface", verified by decompile_function 0x00621E90). `MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` executive summary line 37 corroborates: "generic parent shell background through Background_Overlay @ 0x0072E730". Both docs carry ghidra/verified provenance; the path is active in YR with no TS gate (SHELL_PARENT_BSURFACE doc header: "Active in YR: Yes (standard main-menu shell path, no TS gate)").

The omission is player-visible on every main menu load: the screen region outside the ~632px movie panel and ~168px right panel renders CLEAR_COLOR instead of the retail MNSCRNS/MNSCRNL background art. No refutation is possible — the missing load and draw are unambiguous in the Rust source.

findings.title: Version line pastes VERSION.TXT contents instead of the fixed VersionClass build label
findings.rust_location: src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:281-289 (version_text = format!("{} {}", version_label, state.version_txt)); src/app.rs:663-679 (load_version_txt returns VERSION.TXT trimmed contents when the file exists)
findings.gamemd_behavior: The 0x71D version label is built by VersionClass @0x00A8ECE0 via FUN_0074FAE0. The '%d.%3.3dTUC' format runs UNCONDITIONALLY against the two uint16 build-number fields (this+0x08/+0x0A), both defaulting to 1, producing the literal string '1.001TUC'. The VERSION.TXT file bytes are read into a separate buffer (this+0x2A) and parsed by FUN_0074F760 but are NEVER pasted into the visible label. Final text = '<localized GUI:Version> 1.001TUC'.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust reads VERSION.TXT from disk; if present it uses the file's (trimmed) contents verbatim as the second token. Only when the file is absent does it fall back to '1.001TUC'. With a real retail VERSION.TXT present (which differs from '1.001TUC'), Rust renders a different version string than gamemd shows.
findings.evidence: VERSION_TXT_RESOLUTION_AND_FALLBACK_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §6 'the %d.%3.3dTUC fallback always runs, unconditionally' and §7 (both integers default to 1 → 1.001); MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §0x71D 'always formats the uint16 pair ... (both default to 1, producing "1.001TUC"); the VERSION.TXT bytes go into a separate buffer ... but are not pasted into the version label'

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed at src/app.rs:663-679 and src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:282: Rust reads VERSION.TXT raw text and pastes it verbatim as the second %s token; fallback is "1.001TUC" only when the file is absent.

Gamemd side: VERSION_TXT_RESOLUTION_AND_FALLBACK_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §6 (verified via decompile_function 0x0074FAE0) is definitive — FUN_0074FAE0 always calls FUN_007C8EF4 with "%d.%3.3dTUC" format into this+0x0C; it never pastes raw VERSION.TXT text into the display buffer. The disassembly at MAIN_MENU_TITLE_TEXT_RENDER_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §2 (0x00531fce→0x00531fd5) shows FUN_0074fae0's return value (param_1+0x0C, the numeric string) is what FUN_00735120 widens and sends to control 0x71D. The OWNERDRAW doc §10.3 stating "Final text: VERSION.TXT contents" is an imprecise paraphrase in the docs — the authoritative VERSION_TXT doc §6 corrects this.

So the drift is real: if a VERSION.TXT is present, Rust outputs the raw file text while gamemd outputs the %d.%3.3dTUC-formatted string (potentially using VERSION.TXT-derived numbers if FUN_0074F760 populated the fields first, but still in numeric format, not raw text). The finding's gamemd claim is correct.

Refutation attempted on trigger_frequency: the retail install at C:\Users\enok\Documents\Command and Conquer Red Alert II\ has no VERSION.TXT file (confirmed via Glob). With no VERSION.TXT, both Rust and gamemd produce "1.001TUC" — outputs are identical. The "every-match" frequency label is incorrect; the visible difference only fires when a VERSION.TXT file exists in the game CWD, which is not the case with this retail install. Drift is latent/conditional, not unconditional.

Correction to the finding: the evidence citation from OWNERDRAW doc §10.3 ("VERSION.TXT bytes are not pasted into the version label") is actually correct behavior, but that doc also says the final text IS "VERSION.TXT contents" — a contradiction within the docs. The VERSION_TXT doc §6 is authoritative. The finding's gamemd claim stands; the OWNERDRAW doc's description of the final string is the imprecise one.
verdict_obj.correction: The OWNERDRAW doc §10.3 says the final text is "VERSION.TXT contents" but this is an inaccurate paraphrase. The authoritative source (VERSION_TXT doc §6, verified via decompile_function 0x0074FAE0) confirms gamemd always formats this+0x0C with %d.%3.3dTUC — never raw VERSION.TXT text. The finding's gamemd claim is correct. Additionally, trigger_frequency should be "conditional-on-VERSION.TXT-present" not "every-match" — with no VERSION.TXT in the retail install, both sides output "1.001TUC" and the player sees no difference.

findings.title: No click feedback beyond the instant pressed-frame swap (ButtonFadeEffect family)
findings.rust_location: src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:135-167 (build_button_instances picks one static frame; no per-frame effect list or click animation)
findings.gamemd_behavior: RTTI for VectorClass<ButtonFadeEffect*> and DynamicVectorClass<ButtonFadeEffect*> exist at 0x00820428/0x00820460, but subsequent verification found no live allocation/iterator path in YR; the in-game cameo/tab-flash uses a separate scheduler (FUN_0069DFC0) and CameoEntry.FlashEndFrame is dead. The only verified main-menu button click visual is the instant pressed-frame swap (frame 4) plus +2 px content sink.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust draws the instant pressed-frame swap and +2 px sink, with no extra fade/glow. This MATCHES gamemd's verified behavior; ButtonFadeEffect is dead code in YR. Surfaced as an explicitly-checked non-disparity.
findings.evidence: BUTTON_FADE_EFFECT_VISUAL_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §6 (ButtonFadeEffect/0x4DC mechanism active only for network-lobby buttons; not the in-game/main-menu cameo path; CameoEntry.FlashEndFrame dead); MAIN_MENU_BUTTON_CLICK_ANIMATION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md YELLOW banner ('ButtonFadeEffect is dead WW codebase scaffolding')

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side (src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:135-167): `build_button_instances` picks SDBTNANM frames 2/3/4 from the atlas (button_default / button_hover / button_pressed) with a locally-derived 1 Hz toggle for hover and a +2 px Y sink on press. No click-fade list, no per-frame effect iterator.

Gamemd side — verified via three independent research docs:

1. BUTTON_FADE_EFFECT_STRUCT_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §3-4: `FUN_006071e0` is the animation loop, but it is gated on `record[+0xC1] != 0`. The `DynamicVectorClass<ButtonFadeEffect*>` RTTI containers have NO code xrefs (`get_xrefs_to 0x00820428` and `0x00820460` → zero results). No ButtonFadeEffect instance allocation was found anywhere in the button-click call chains. The local `operator_new` inside FUN_006071e0 allocates only a per-button timing array, not ButtonFadeEffect structs.

2. SHELL_TRANSITION_ON_MAIN_MENU_CLICK_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §2: Exhaustive byte-pattern search across all 8 register encodings for `MOV byte ptr [reg+0xBD], 1` returns exactly ONE match — at `0x006083D3` inside `FUN_00608380`. That setter's only caller is `CDFileClass__Constructor` (Load/Save success branch). No code path from dialog 0xE2's WM_INITDIALOG, dialog proc, or main-menu launcher reaches the setter. `FUN_00608070` (slide-out trigger) bails immediately on `record[+0xC1] == 0` for dialog 0xE2. Conclusion: `FUN_006071e0` never fires on a main-menu button click.

3. BUTTON_FADE_EFFECT_VISUAL_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §6: The 1 Hz SDBTNANM frame 2↔3 toggle (`0x4DC` / `SetTimer`) fires only for network-lobby owner-drawn buttons (six `push 0x4DC` sites, all in `FUN_005e2340`, `FUN_005e23a0`, `FUN_007a2750`, `FUN_007a27d0` referencing `netdlg2`/`wonline`). None are in main-menu or in-game sidebar code. `CameoEntry.FlashEndFrame` is confirmed dead (no non-zero writer — per CAMEO_FLASH_END_FRAME_WRITER_GHIDRA_REPORT.md).

Net gamemd behavior for a main-menu click: WM_LBUTTONDOWN → click sound (`VocClass__PlayAtPos`) + frame-4 SHP snap + +2 px Y shift → WM_COMMAND → dialog destroyed instantly (no slide, no fade, no ButtonFadeEffect). Rust matches this exactly. The finding's gamemd characterization is correct. The non-disparity label is justified.

findings.title: Seventh owner-draw button (Yuri Website, 0x55F) is not laid out or rendered
findings.rust_location: src/ui/main_menu_shell/layout.rs:305-330 (buttons array has exactly 6 entries; YuriWebsite control absent); src/ui/main_menu_shell/state.rs:8-16 (MainMenuControlId::YuriWebsite0x71b defined but unused in layout); src/ui/main_menu_shell/layout.rs:296,304 (website_static rect computed but never pushed to any draw list)
findings.gamemd_behavior: FUN_00608CD0 matches control 0x55F (Yuri Website) for dialog 0xE2 alongside the six menu buttons, and FUN_006040B0 maps 0x55F to STT:MainButtonYuriWebSite. The dispatch doc lists 0x55F as a 7th owner-draw button routed through the SDBTNANM (+0xB0=1) path. Whether it has a visible non-zero rect on the standard YR 0xE2 (vs being hidden) is not conclusively established in the composition resource table, which lists only 6 buttons + statics.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust enumerates only 6 buttons and never renders any website control; the YuriWebsite0x71b enum variant and website_static rect exist but are dead. If retail shows a visible website button/icon (e.g. corner static 0x71C/0x55F), Rust omits it.
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_BUTTON_DISPATCH_LAB_0060A330_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §1.3 (FUN_00608CD0 matches 0x55F for 0xE2) and §1.5 ('0x55F Yuri Website ... SDBTNANM frames 2/3/4'); MAIN_MENU_HOVER_MESSAGES doc §5 (FUN_006040B0 maps 0x55F→STT:MainButtonYuriWebSite for 0xE2). Composition doc §0x71C states no proven visible output for that corner static — visibility of 0x55F itself is unresolved.

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed: `layout.rs:78` declares `buttons: [MainMenuButtonRect; 6]` with no `0x55F` entry; `layout.rs:304` computes `website_static` but it is never consumed in `app_main_menu_shell_render.rs` (confirmed by grep — only three layout.rs references). `state.rs:15` defines `YuriWebsite0x71b` but it is never pushed to the buttons array.

Gamemd side: The RT_DIALOG `0xE2` resource table in `MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §Dialog and Child Controls` (verified via `FUN_00531CC0` decompilation) lists exactly 11 child controls: `0x694`, `0x695`, `0x71A`, `0x71C`, `0x71D`, `0x683`, `0x684`, `0x578`, `0x686`, `0x55C`, `0x3EE`. Control `0x55F` is absent from this resource table. The same doc §0x71C confirms: "No `push 0x55F` GetDlgItem/ShowWindow-style use was found in the binary scan."

The dispatch doc `MAIN_MENU_BUTTON_DISPATCH_LAB_0060A330_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §1.3` does confirm `FUN_00608CD0` would match `0x55F` for dialog `0xE2`, but this predicate only applies to child windows created by the dialog resource. Since `0x55F` is not in the RT_DIALOG `0xE2` resource, no child window with that ID is ever created, and `EnumChildWindows` never encounters it. The predicate match is irrelevant — the control does not exist in this dialog.

The finding's gamemd claim is therefore mis-stated: `0x55F` is in `FUN_00608CD0`'s match table for `0xE2`, but it is not instantiated as a child of dialog `0xE2`. Rust correctly renders 6 buttons and no website control. The `website_static` dead field and `YuriWebsite0x71b` dead enum variant are pre-built stubs that are never rendered, which matches gamemd's observable output of 6 buttons with no website element on the main menu.
verdict_obj.correction: The finding mis-states the gamemd side: `FUN_00608CD0` matching `0x55F` for dialog `0xE2` does NOT mean a website button is rendered. Control `0x55F` is not present in the RT_DIALOG `0xE2` resource definition (verified via `FUN_00531CC0` decompilation and the composition doc's child control table). No child window with ID `0x55F` is created for dialog `0xE2`, so the `LAB_0060A330` callback never sets `+0xB0` on any `0x55F` record, and no button is drawn. The Rust port's 6-button layout without any website control exactly matches gamemd's observable output.

This path is reached via `render_egui_main_menu_fallback` (app.rs:696-724), called when `main_menu_shell_failed` is true or `main_menu_shell_chrome` is None (app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:393-395). The failure flag is set at startup when `startup_asset_manager.is_none() || main_menu_shell_chrome.is_none()` (app.rs:2013-2014). The `main_menu_show_skirmish_setup` branch at app.rs:2352 is dead code — that field is initialized false and never set to true anywhere in the codebase. findings.title: Loading screen uses an egui rounded-rect overlay with custom English strings, not gamemd's loading composition
findings.rust_location: src/ui/main_menu.rs:300-391 (draw_loading_screen: rounded dark overlay rect, 'Mission deployment' / 'Loading...' / 'Map: ...' / 'Parsing map...' proportional-font text; loading_screen_image() always returns None)
findings.gamemd_behavior: gamemd's load sequence draws a full-screen load screen image with CSF-localized text, not an egui rounded card with literal English developer strings. (Exact gamemd loading-screen composition not re-investigated in this audit.)
findings.rust_behavior: Rust paints a dark rounded overlay box with hardcoded English strings over a (currently always-absent) loading image, using egui proportional fonts rather than GAME.FNT and CSF strings.
findings.evidence: src/ui/main_menu.rs:388-391 (loading_screen_image returns None — no image ever drawn); strings are literal English at lines 343,351,358,365. gamemd side not established from a loading-screen-specific doc.

verdict_obj.reasoning: Rust side confirmed at src/ui/main_menu.rs:300-391: draws a rounded dark egui overlay (430x132 px, 18px radius, rgba 9/14/20/214) with four hardcoded English strings ("Mission deployment" at line 344, "Loading..." at 351, "Map: {name}" at 358, "Parsing map..." at 365) using egui proportional fonts. loading_screen_image() at lines 388-391 unconditionally returns None so no image is ever drawn. gamemd side is thoroughly established by LOADING_SCREEN_WM_PAINT_MODE2_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (verified at 0x00621E90, PUDLGBG_LOADING_SCREEN_SHP_LIFECYCLE_GHIDRA_REPORT.md, and three related reports): WM_PAINT mode-2 draws frame 0 of a side-selected PUDLGBG*.SHP (Allied/Soviet/Yuri/Neutral) at position (0,0) via CC_Draw_Shape with no text, no progress overlay, no English strings. The branch contains zero text draw calls between 0x006221F4 and 0x006222AD (verified negative in the coverage ledger). The finding's stated uncertainty about gamemd is unwarranted — the binary evidence is high-confidence and directly contradicts the Rust output. No refutation is possible: the outputs differ fundamentally (SHP full-screen frame vs egui rounded card with English literals).
verdict_obj.correction: The finding labels the gamemd side as "not re-investigated in this audit" and confidence as "uncertain," but multiple Ghidra-verified reports (LOADING_SCREEN_WM_PAINT_MODE2_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md at 0x00621E90, PUDLGBG_LOADING_SCREEN_SHP_LIFECYCLE_GHIDRA_REPORT.md, LOADING_PROGRESS_CALLBACK_VISIBLE_UI_GHIDRA_REPORT.md) fully establish it. The gamemd side description in the finding is correct; only the confidence label should be HIGH, not uncertain.

findings.title: Button artwork source (SDBTNANM frames 2/3/4 + SDBTNANM.PAL) is correct
findings.rust_location: src/render/main_menu_shell_chrome.rs:55-65 (SDBTNANM.SHP frames 2/3/4 via SDBTNANM.PAL); src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:54-66 (frame 2 default / 3 hover / 4 pressed)
findings.gamemd_behavior: LAB_0060A330 writes record+0xB0=1 for all 6 main-menu buttons, forcing the iVar14==1 branch of OwnerDraw_Button_00612B70: g_SDBTNANM_SHP frame 2 default, frame 3 focus/hover (when +0xC5 set), frame 4 pressed, drawn via CC_Draw_Shape with the SDBTNANM palette convert (FUN_0072E2C0). The greyscale bue_*30/bde_*30 PCX path is NEVER reached on dialog 0xE2.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust loads SDBTNANM frames 2/3/4 with SDBTNANM.PAL (fallback SHELL2.PAL) and selects them exactly as gamemd does. This matches; surfaced as a verified non-disparity (earlier docs that claimed PCX were refuted).
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_BUTTON_DISPATCH_LAB_0060A330_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §1.5 + §3 ('chrome atlas comment ... is correct'); SHELL_BUTTON_GREYSCALE_COLORIZATION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md YELLOW banner (PCX path refuted for 0xE2)

verdict_obj.reasoning:
The finding claims Rust "selects them exactly as gamemd does" — specifically that frame 3 (hover) appears when button_object+0xC5 is set. This is wrong for dialog 0xE2.

Asset source is correct: MAIN_MENU_BUTTON_DISPATCH_LAB_0060A330_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §1.1 confirms LAB_0060A330 sets +0xB0=1 for all dialog 0xE2 buttons, routing them to the iVar14==1 branch in OwnerDraw_Button_00612B70, which loads g_SDBTNANM_SHP with FUN_0072E2C0 palette. Frames 2/3/4 and SDBTNANM.PAL are correct.

Hover frame selection is WRONG: HOVER_DISPATCHER_FUN_007B66C0_FAMILY_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §3.1–3.3 enumerates all 6 push-0x4DC sites in the binary (search_byte_patterns "68 DC 04 00 00" → 6 hits; decompile_function on all 4 named functions + read_memory at 2 inline sites). Every single one calls GetDlgItem(_, 0x59F) — a network dialog control — not any dialog 0xE2 button. The 0x4DC message is never sent to dialog 0xE2 buttons. Without 0x4DC, no SetTimer is armed, +0xC5 never toggles via WM_TIMER (0x113), and frame 3 is never selected. In gamemd, dialog 0xE2 buttons display frame 2 (default) until pressed (frame 4); frame 3 is never visible.

Rust drift: src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:150-153 implements a 1000ms elapsed-time toggle (now.duration_since(start).as_millis() / 1000 % 2 == 1) that selects frame 3 (button_hover) after 1 second of hover. This behavior does not exist in gamemd for dialog 0xE2. The Rust port shows a hover highlight frame on main-menu buttons; gamemd does not.

The finding mis-states gamemd behavior: "frame 3 focus/hover (when +0xC5 set)" — +0xC5 is NEVER set for dialog 0xE2 buttons because the 0x4DC trigger never fires there.

Note: The older MAIN_MENU_HOVER_MESSAGES_0x4DC_0x4E8_0x4E9_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (same date, earlier timestamp 18:00 vs DISPATCH 18:48) incorrectly claimed 0x4DC is sent to dialog 0xE2 via FUN_006040B0; HOVER_DISPATCHER explicitly refutes this with byte-level search evidence.
verdict_obj.correction: gamemd side mis-stated: the finding claims frame 3 appears when +0xC5 is set on hover (implying 0x4DC reaches dialog 0xE2 buttons). It does not — all 6 push-0x4DC sites target GetDlgItem(_, 0x59F) (a network dialog control). For dialog 0xE2, +0xC5 is never toggled and frame 3 is never selected. Rust shows frame 3 after 1 second hover; gamemd always shows frame 2 (or frame 4 if pressed). Asset source (SDBTNANM SHP + palette) is correct; hover frame selection is drift.

findings.title: SDBTNANM.PAL falls back to SHELL2.PAL when missing, shifting button colors
findings.gamemd_behavior: The SDBTNANM button artwork is colorized through the dedicated SDBTNANM.PAL convert (DAT_00B0FBDC), producing the red/orange/yellow gradient. gamemd has no SHELL2.PAL substitution for this path; if SDBTNANM.PAL is genuinely present in retail archives it is always used.
findings.rust_behavior: If SDBTNANM.PAL fails to load, Rust colorizes the SDBTNANM frames with SHELL2.PAL instead, which would render the gradient in different colors than retail. Retail ships SDBTNANM.PAL, so this only fires on a load failure, but the fallback colorization is not gamemd's behavior.
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md right-panel draw stack table (SDBTNANM uses SDBTNANM.PAL / DAT_00B0FBDC); palette string block lists SDBTNANM.PAL, SHELL2.PAL, SHELL.PAL as distinct converts

verdict_obj.reasoning: Rust lines 58-59 of src/render/main_menu_shell_chrome.rs confirm the fallback exactly as described: `load_named_palette(assets, "SDBTNANM.PAL").or_else(|| load_named_palette(assets, "SHELL2.PAL"))`. The gamemd side was verified through MAIN_MENU_BUTTON_DISPATCH_LAB_0060A330_GHIDRA_REPORT.md and SHELL_BUTTON_GREYSCALE_COLORIZATION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §2.7: the iVar14==1 branch in OwnerDraw_Button_00612B70 calls FUN_0072e2c0() which returns the SDBTNANM.PAL ConvertClass at DAT_00b0fbdc. This is a distinct pointer from the SHELL2.PAL ConvertClass at DAT_00b0fbd4 (loaded by FUN_0072aa40 during Init_Game). gamemd has no SHELL2.PAL substitution logic anywhere in this path — the two ConvertClass slots are independent globals. On a stock install SDBTNANM.PAL is present in the palette string block at 0x00845438 and always loads, so the fallback code path is never exercised in normal play. However the fallback is structurally different from gamemd (which would dereference a null ConvertClass or produce a crash rather than silently substitute SHELL2.PAL), making this confirmed drift under the CLAUDE.md burden of proof — exact equivalence is unproven for the failure case and the fallback colorization is demonstrably wrong (SHELL2.PAL produces different colors than SDBTNANM.PAL for the gradient frames). The finding's gamemd claim is correctly stated.

findings.title: Responsive layout stretches the 800x600 shell to fill the window, diverging from gamemd's center-no-scale rule
findings.rust_location: src/ui/main_menu_shell/layout.rs:345-380 (compute_responsive_layout scales base shell by screen_w/800, screen_h/600 independently)
findings.gamemd_behavior: gamemd does NOT scale child controls or the movie to fill the window. RightPanel__ComputeLayoutRects only applies a centering margin (screen_w-800)/2 when screen_w>1023 and (screen_h-600)/2 when screen_h>767; otherwise rects stay at native pixel sizes anchored to the edges. The movie stays 632x570 (or 472x450), centered on oversized screens, never stretched.
findings.rust_behavior: compute_responsive_layout (a code path the renderer can select) stretches movie, buttons, right panel, lower strip, and text rects by independent X/Y scale factors, intentionally drifting from retail pixel parity. compute_layout (the non-responsive path) matches gamemd; the responsive variant does not.
findings.evidence: src/ui/main_menu_shell/layout.rs:345-350 doc comment ('This intentionally drifts from retail pixel parity'); MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md 'Layout and Resolution Behavior' (RightPanel__ComputeLayoutRects centering thresholds 1023/767, no per-control scale)

verdict_obj.reasoning: Checked every call site of `compute_responsive_layout` across the entire `src/` tree. It is called in exactly two places: the unit tests at `src/ui/main_menu_shell/layout.rs:452` and `:464`. No production code path invokes it — `app.rs` (lines 1449, 1471, 1808) and `app_main_menu_shell_render.rs` (lines 324, 411) import and call only `compute_layout`. The render glue file `app_main_menu_shell_render.rs` line 15 explicitly names `compute_layout` in its import list, not `compute_responsive_layout`. The function is exported from `mod.rs:8` but has zero live callers outside the test module. The finding's claim that "the renderer can select" the responsive path is incorrect — no renderer selects it. The doc comment at lines 348–350 documents the function's intended semantics ("intentionally drifts from retail pixel parity"), but that description applies to a dead-code function. The production render path uses `compute_layout` exclusively, which the finding itself concedes matches gamemd. No player-visible output diverges.
verdict_obj.correction: The finding mis-states the Rust side: `compute_responsive_layout` has no live caller in the render or input pipeline. It is a test-only function. The "renderer can select" framing is false. The production code path is `compute_layout` at every call site in `app.rs` and `app_main_menu_shell_render.rs`, which the finding concedes matches gamemd behavior.

findings.title: Button SHP art is right-anchored and stretched to the tile, not drawn at native frame size centered in the 162px client
findings.rust_location: src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:78-99 (push_button_shp scales the SHP frame by rect.w/168 and rect.h/42, then right-anchors x = rect.x + (rect.w - frame_w))
findings.gamemd_behavior: OwnerDraw_Button_00612B70 draws the SDBTNANM frame via CC_Draw_Shape into the button client at the SHP's native frame size, vertically centered in the 37px client (the 30px art family is centered in the 37px client per the visual-assets doc). The art is not stretched to a 168x42 chrome tile, and horizontal placement follows the button rect, not a right-edge anchor against the 168px sidebar tile.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust scales the SDBTNANM frame to fill (rect.w/RIGHT_PANEL_WIDTH=168) x (rect.h/RIGHT_PANEL_TILE_H=42) and right-anchors it against the tile right edge with a 12px left bevel. This both rescales the native art and uses a right-anchor offset that gamemd's owner-draw button paint does not apply, so button art size/position can differ by pixels from retail.
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_VISUAL_ASSETS_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §6 ('the selected 30 px art strip is vertically centered in the 37 px client'); MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md button-rendering section (art centered in client, no tile-stretch). Button client is 162x37 (DLU 108x23), not the 168x42 chrome tile Rust scales against.

verdict_obj.reasoning:
**Rust side confirmed (src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:78-99):**
`push_button_shp` draws SDBTNANM.SHP frames 2/3/4 for the main-menu buttons, scaled by `rect.w / RIGHT_PANEL_WIDTH` (168) and `rect.h / RIGHT_PANEL_TILE_H` (42), then right-anchors the art with `x = rect.x + (rect.w - frame_w)`. The SDBTNANM canvas is 156x42 (verified via `RIGHT_PANEL_SHP_HEADER_DIMENSIONS_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §2`); scaling against 168x42 produces a frame_w = 156*(162/168) ≈ 150px and applies a 12px left-gap right-anchor offset.

**gamemd side — finding partially mis-stated:**
The finding claims gamemd draws "the SDBTNANM frame via CC_Draw_Shape into the button client at the SHP's native frame size, vertically centered in the 37px client." This is wrong in a critical detail: `OwnerDraw_Button_00612B70` draws SDBTNANM frames only when `state+0xB0 == 1` (the right-panel snap path). The main-menu dialog-0xE2 buttons follow the **PCX path** (`state+0xB0 == 0`), which blits three PCX pieces (`bue_li30.pcx`, `bue_mi30.pcx`, `bue_ri30.pcx`) at native size. This is confirmed by:
- `BUTTON_FADE_EFFECT_VISUAL_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §3`: the SDBTNANM branch is gated on `iVar14 == 1` (fade type = 1).
- `SKIRMISH_OWNERDRAW_BUTTON_GEOMETRY_DOC_RECONCILIATION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §7 asset role matrix`: "SDBTNANM.SHP: drawn: no for these button faces … face path inactive — `state+0xB0 == 0` PCX path."
- `MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §Button Rendering`: "the selected 30 px art strip is vertically centered in the 37 px client; the middle PCX is tiled by `FUN_006BA3E0`, not stretched."

**Correction to the finding's gamemd claim:**
gamemd does NOT draw any SDBTNANM frame for main-menu buttons. It draws three PCX pieces (`bue/bde_li/mi/ri30.pcx`) at native size (30px height for released, 27px for pressed), tiled in the middle, vertically centered in the 37px client. The button rect is 162x37 px (not 162x42 — that is the SDBTNBKGD tile height, which is a background chrome element, not the button control).

**Drift severity:**
Rust is using the wrong asset family entirely (SDBTNANM SHP instead of PCX pieces) and applies wrong scaling and anchoring on top. The SDBTNANM frame drawn at ~150x42 scaled output versus the correct 30px-high PCX-piece-at-native-size (centered in 37px client) is a visible pixel mismatch on every main-menu button, every frame. Drift is confirmed and larger than the finding states: the wrong asset is drawn, not merely wrong-sized/anchored.
verdict_obj.correction: The finding's gamemd claim is wrong: gamemd does NOT draw SDBTNANM frames for main-menu buttons. `OwnerDraw_Button_00612B70` draws SDBTNANM only when `state+0xB0 == 1`; main-menu dialog-0xE2 buttons use `state+0xB0 == 0` (the default PCX path), which blits `bue_li30.pcx / bue_mi30.pcx / bue_ri30.pcx` at native size (30px released, 27px pressed), vertically centered in the 37px client. The button client is 162x37 px, not 162x42 px (42 is the SDBTNBKGD background tile height). The scaling-and-anchoring drift the finding describes is real, but it is a downstream symptom of using the wrong asset family entirely.

findings.title: Button hit/draw rect is snapped to the 168x42 chrome tile instead of the 162x37 DLU client rect
findings.rust_location: src/ui/main_menu_shell/layout.rs:267-281 (button_rect_for_dlu_y snaps to nearest tile row and sets rect = tile rect 168x42); layout.rs:305-330
findings.gamemd_behavior: The six buttons are Win32 owner-draw controls at DLU 425,Y,108,23 → pixel ~162x37, at distinct Y values (203/247/291/335/379/536 at 800x600). They are not snapped onto a 42px tile grid; the SDBTNBKGD tile (168x42) is a separate background element drawn behind them.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust replaces each button's true 162x37 client rect with the nearest 168x42 SDBTNBKGD tile rect, so button width (168 vs 162), height (42 vs 37), and Y positions (tile-snapped 199/241/283/... vs 203/247/291/...) all differ from gamemd. This also shifts hit-test boundaries.
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md control table (0x683 px 638,203,162,37 etc.); layout.rs:393 test asserts buttons[0] = (632,199,168,42), differing from the documented (638,203,162,37) client rect

verdict_obj.reasoning: Read layout.rs:267-281 — `button_rect_for_dlu_y` explicitly snaps each button's Y to the nearest SDBTNBKGD tile row and returns the full tile rect (168x42) as the button rect. The test at layout.rs:393 asserts `buttons[0].rect = RectPx::new(632, 199, 168, 42)`. The research doc `MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` (line 65, verified 2026-05-17 via Ghidra decompilation) records button 0x683 at DLU `425,125,108,23` → pixel `(638, 203, 162, 37)`. The same doc (lines 180-198) confirms SDBTNBKGD is a background SHP drawn by `RightPanel__Draw @ 0x0072E450` and is separate from the owner-draw button controls. The four concrete differences at 800x600: X offset 632 vs 638 (+6 px), Y offset 199 vs 203 (+4 px), width 168 vs 162 (+6 px), height 42 vs 37 (+5 px). These shift both the draw rect and the hit-test boundary for every menu button on every frame the main menu is visible. No algebraic equivalence, no bit-identical trace, and no exhaustive caller check exists to downgrade this — the test itself encodes the wrong values.

findings.title: Buttons/title anchored at x=632 (sidebar tile left) vs gamemd's DLU-derived ~638 client x
findings.gamemd_behavior: The owner-draw button client rect left edge is DLU 425 → MulDiv(425,6,4) = 638 px at 800x600 (composition doc explicitly notes '425 DLU maps to 638 px under MulDiv, not 637'). The 162px-wide button sits at x=638, leaving the 168px SDBTNBKGD tile (x=632) to extend ~6px further left as bevel.
findings.rust_behavior: Because Rust makes the button rect equal to the 168px tile rect (x=632), the button art/label region starts at x=632 instead of 638, a 6px leftward shift of the entire button column relative to gamemd.
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md 'Tiny layout detail: 425 DLU maps to 638 px under Win32 MulDiv, not 637'; layout.rs:393 (button x=632)

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side verified at layout.rs:280 — `button_rect_for_dlu_y` returns `RectPx::new(right_panel.tile.x, y, right_panel.tile.w, tile_h)` where `right_panel.tile.x = effective_right - RIGHT_PANEL_WIDTH = 800 - 168 = 632` and `right_panel.tile.w = 168`. So at 800x600, every button is placed at x=632, w=168. Confirmed.

Gamemd side verified via MAIN_MENU_DIALOG_0XE2_FULL_VISIBLE_COMPOSITION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md: The RT_DIALOG resource creates button controls at DLU rect (425, N, 108, 23). The doc states at line 72 "425 DLU maps to 638 px under Win32 MulDiv, not 637" and the table (lines 65-70) lists "approx px rect" as (638, ..., 162, 37) for all six buttons. Win32 MapDialogRect / MulDiv(425,6,4) = (425*6+2)/4 = 638, w = MulDiv(108,6,4) = 162. So gamemd creates buttons at x=638, w=162.

The child reposition pass (ResizeShellChildControl_0060C0C0, doc lines 399-417) is documented only to reposition three controls: 0x695 (tooltip), 0x71D (version line), and 0x694 (heading). The six buttons (0x683, 0x684, 0x578, 0x686, 0x55C, 0x3EE) are not listed as being moved by any helper. Therefore buttons remain at their Win32-created x=638, w=162 positions in gamemd.

Note on the finding's gamemd claim: the finding correctly states x=638 from DLU 425 via MulDiv. This is confirmed. However the doc also shows that 0x71D (version line) gets repositioned to x=635 by FUN_0060B610's inset formula (800-3-162=635), NOT to 638 — so the raw DLU position is corrected for statics but not necessarily for buttons. The finding's claim of x=638 for buttons is consistent with the doc's table and the absence of any button-specific repositioning helper.

The drift: Rust x=632 w=168 vs gamemd x=638 w=162. That is a 6px leftward shift of the button column (hit-test region and any client-rect-relative art centering) and a 6px wider click target. The finding is correctly characterized.

findings.title: Title/version/button text color encoded as #FFFF00 (yellow); gamemd RGB-byte-order interpretation has a residual yellow-vs-cyan ambiguity
findings.rust_location: src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:22 (SHELL_BUTTON_TEXT_RGB_FFFF00 = [1,1,0] yellow), used for title/version/tooltip/button labels
findings.gamemd_behavior: DAT_00AC18A4 = 0x0000FFFF is passed to FUN_00621040, which converts it via a byte-order permutation. Docs name the visible result yellow #FFFF00 (consistent with retail observation), but note a residual ambiguity: a strict 0x00RRGGBB read of 0x0000FFFF would be cyan. Working assumption is yellow.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust uses yellow #FFFF00 for all shell text, matching the documented visible result. Surfaced because the gamemd color conversion is only MEDIUM-confidence on RGB byte order (final-pixel capture would settle it); if gamemd actually renders cyan, Rust would drift.
findings.evidence: MAIN_MENU_TITLE_TEXT_RENDER_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §6 confidence table ('Color is #FFFF00 yellow — HIGH content, MEDIUM RGB-byte-order') and §8 open question (yellow vs cyan)

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed: `src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:22` defines `SHELL_BUTTON_TEXT_RGB_FFFF00 = [1.0, 1.0, 0.0]` (R=1.0, G=1.0, B=0.0 = yellow #FFFF00).

gamemd side confirmed via `FUN_00621040_RGB_BYTE_PERMUTATION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` (all three confidence axes: content/identity/binding HIGH, all sourced from live `disassemble_function(0x00621040)` this session): the byte-extraction sequence at `0x00621054` (`MOV BL, AH` → byte 1 = G), `0x00621068` (`AND EAX, 0xFF` → byte 0 = R), and `0x0062106d` (`SHR ESI, 0x10` → byte 2 = B) proves the convention is `0x00BBGGRR`. Under this convention `DAT_00AC18A4 = 0x0000FFFF` → R=255, G=255, B=0.

Full formula applied for RGB565 (RShift=11/RLoss=3, GShift=5/GLoss=2, BShift=0/BLoss=3, confirmed from `DSurface__Constructor @ 0x004ba9d0`): packed16 = 0xF800 | 0x07E0 | 0x0000 = 0xFFE0 = yellow #FFFF00. Same result for RGB555. Both modes produce identical yellow output.

The open question (yellow vs cyan) cited in `MAIN_MENU_TITLE_TEXT_RENDER_GHIDRA_REPORT.md §8` was resolved in the dedicated follow-up doc `FUN_00621040_RGB_BYTE_PERMUTATION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` (OQ1, OQ2, OQ3 all marked RESOLVED with disassembly evidence). The finding is based on a stale open-question that no longer exists. Rust output is bit-identical to gamemd output.
verdict_obj.correction: The finding states the ambiguity is unresolved and confidence is only MEDIUM on RGB byte-order. This is stale: `FUN_00621040_RGB_BYTE_PERMUTATION_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` fully resolves OQ1/OQ2/OQ3 with direct disassembly at `0x00621054`, `0x00621068`, `0x0062106d`. The byte order is confirmed `0x00BBGGRR`; `0x0000FFFF` is definitively yellow in both RGB565 and RGB555. There is no residual ambiguity and no drift.

findings.title: Exit Game skips the GUI:ExitAreYouSure confirm dialog and quits immediately
findings.gamemd_behavior: Clicking Exit (control 0x3EE, return code 6) routes to Main_Game case 6, which loads CSF strings GUI:ExitAreYouSure / TXT_OK / GUI:Cancel and pops a modal CSF message-box (RT_DIALOG template 0x120, helper FUN_005D3490) with a prompt and OK/Cancel owner-draw buttons. Only the OK (TXT_OK) branch (FUN_005D3490 returns 0) proceeds to shutdown; Cancel (returns 1) returns to the menu. Shutdown then writes ra2md.ini via OptionsClass__WriteToINI, fades to black, pumps vox up to 3s, and returns 0.
findings.rust_behavior: MainMenuShellAction::ExitGame calls event_loop.exit() directly with no confirmation dialog, no CSF prompt, no options persistence, and no fade/vox-pump shutdown sequence.
findings.evidence: QUIT_CONFIRM_DIALOG_MAIN_MENU_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (case-6 dispatch at 0x0052DDBA, FUN_005D3490 template 0x120, shutdown case-7); Rust src/app.rs:1632 `MainMenuShellAction::ExitGame => event_loop.exit()`.

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side (src/app.rs:1632): `MainMenuShellAction::ExitGame => event_loop.exit()` — direct exit, no confirmation dialog, no CSF prompt, no options write, no shutdown sequence. Confirmed by grep across all of src/: zero matches for ExitAreYouSure, exit_confirm, or quit_confirm anywhere in the codebase.

gamemd side: Verified via QUIT_CONFIRM_DIALOG_MAIN_MENU_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (ghidra/verified) and corroborated by MAIN_GAME_STATE_MACHINE_CASES_GHIDRA_REPORT.md. Button 0x3EE on dialog 0xE2 returns code 6 from FUN_00531CC0; case-6 at 0x0052DDBA calls FUN_005D3490 with CSF keys GUI:ExitAreYouSure / TXT_OK / GUI:Cancel (RT_DIALOG template 0x120). Return 0 (OK) → ESI=7 → case-7 shutdown (OptionsClass__WriteToINI at 0x005FAD10, music stop, vox pump up to 3s, clean return); return 1 (Cancel) → ESI=0x12 → back to main menu.

No refutation is possible. The Rust code correctly identifies the button (ExitGame0x3ee maps to return code 6 in state.rs:64) but skips the entire case-6 confirm flow. The finding's facts on both sides are accurate. Player-visible: every quit attempt exits without the expected "Are you sure?" prompt and without persisting options — fires every time the player exits via the menu button.

findings.title: Options button is a no-op; the entire options launcher dialog (case 5) is unimplemented
findings.gamemd_behavior: Options (control 0x55C, return code 5) → Main_Game case 5 → OptionsClass__ShowLauncherDialog (0x0055FC80) which opens RT_DIALOG 0xD5 (proc 0x0055FDB0): difficulty selector 0x50F, game-speed slider 0x52B, scroll-rate slider 0x52A, sound 0x52F / score 0x532 / voice 0x536 volume sliders, health-bars checkbox 0x601, action-lines checkbox 0x602, resolution combo 0x6ED, plus OK(0x5CB)/resolution-sub-dialog(0x5CD)/advanced(0x5CE) results. Backs up 0xB8 bytes of OptionsClass for cancel-restore and writes ra2md.ini on accept.

findings.evidence: OPTIONS_DIALOG_CASE5_AND_FIELD_MAP_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (case 5 → 0x0055FC80, dialog 0xD5, control IDs, return codes); Rust src/app.rs:1639-1645 catch-all log-only arm.

gamemd side confirmed via OPTIONS_DIALOG_CASE5_AND_FIELD_MAP_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (2026-05-19, source: ghidra/verified), itself backed by `decompile_function 0x0052d9a0` (Main_Game case 5 → OptionsClass__ShowLauncherDialog at 0x0055FC80) and `disassemble_function 0x0055fc80` (dialog resource 0xD5, proc 0x0055FDB0). findings.title: Movies & Credits button is a no-op; case-4 sub-panel, movie picker, and credits roll are unimplemented
findings.gamemd_behavior: Movies & Credits (control 0x686, return code 4) → Main_Game case 4 opens intermediate dialog 0x101 (proc 0x0052D790) with three owner-draw buttons: Sneak Preview (0x68D→0xD), Movies (0x68E→0xE), Credits (0x68F→0xF) plus Back (0x686→0x12). findings.title: Single Player / Movies submenu entry does not reposition+restart the left RA2TS intro movie
findings.gamemd_behavior: Main_Game case 1 (and case 4) call FUN_0060D380 with param_1=1, which invokes FUN_0052b9b0(hwnd): it GetDlgItem(0x71A) on the movie panel, SetWindowPos to the centered origin ((w-800)/2,(h-600)/2), then SendMessageA(0x4e3, wParam=1) (restart/play) and SendMessageA(0x4e4, 0, name) selecting Ra2ts_s for 640-wide else Ra2ts_l. Net effect: the shared left intro movie is re-armed and restarts from its start when the submenu opens.
findings.rust_behavior: render_single_player_shell reuses the shared state.main_menu_movie and only steps it by elapsed time (movie.step(...)); it never restarts playback or re-issues a play/reset on submenu entry. The intro movie continues from wherever the main-menu left it.
findings.evidence: decompile_function 0x0060D380 (param_1 gate → FUN_0052b9b0); decompile_function 0x0052b9b0 (SendMessage 0x4e3 wParam=1 restart + 0x4e4 name set); read_memory 0x825ce0 = 'Ra2ts_l\0'/'Ra2ts_s\0'. Rust src/app_single_player_shell_render.rs:305-312 steps shared movie without reset.

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Verified both sides directly.

**gamemd side (binary):** `decompile_function 0x0060D380` confirms the function takes a `char param_1` and calls `FUN_0052b9b0()` only when `param_1 != '\0'`. `decompile_function 0x0052b9b0` confirms it does exactly what the finding claims: `GetDlgItem(hwnd, 0x71A)`, `SetWindowPos` to `((g_ScreenWidth-800)/2, (g_ScreenHeight-600)/2)`, then `SendMessageA(hWnd, 0x4e3, 1, 0)` (restart/play) and `SendMessageA(hWnd, 0x4e4, 0, 0x825ce0/0x825ce8)` (name select Ra2ts_l/Ra2ts_s based on screen width). `Main_Game` (verified via `decompile_function 0x0052d9a0`) at switch case 1 calls `FUN_0060d380(1)` and case 4 also calls `FUN_0060d380(1)` — both pass param_1=1, so `FUN_0052b9b0` fires unconditionally on Single Player submenu entry.

**Rust side:** `open_single_player_shell` at `src/app.rs:536-547` resets UI state only — no movie restart, no seek-to-zero, no play signal. `render_single_player_shell` at `src/app_single_player_shell_render.rs:305-312` only calls `movie.step(&state.gpu, elapsed)` on the shared `main_menu_movie` — it advances the movie by elapsed wall time with no reset. There is no restart on submenu entry anywhere in the call path.

The drift is real: gamemd re-arms and restarts the Ra2TS intro movie every time the Single Player (or Movies/case-4) submenu opens; Rust lets it run continuously from wherever the main-menu left it. Observable to any player who idles on the main menu before clicking Single Player.

findings.title: SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU doc misattributes case-1 param_1=1 as 'EVA welcome-back audio'
findings.gamemd_behavior: FUN_0060D380's param_1=1 branch calls FUN_0052b9b0, which is the movie-panel reposition+restart routine (SetWindowPos + SendMessage 0x4e3/0x4e4 to control 0x71A), NOT an EVA/welcome audio call. There is no audio API call on this path.
findings.rust_behavior: open_single_player_shell plays no audio, which is coincidentally correct for the (wrong) EVA claim, but it also omits the actual movie restart (see mm-dialogs-sp-submenu-movie-no-restart). Flagged because the source doc's claim is misleading and could drive a wrong fix (adding EVA audio that gamemd never plays).
findings.evidence: SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU_DIALOG_CASE1_GHIDRA_REPORT.md:18 (' ; param_1 = 1 (triggers EVA welcome-back audio)'); contradicted by decompile_function 0x0052b9b0 (movie panel reposition/restart, no audio).

verdict_obj.reasoning: Checked the Rust side at app.rs:536-547 (`open_single_player_shell`): no audio is played, confirmed. Checked the gamemd side by decompiling `FUN_0052B9B0` at 0x0052B9B0 live via Ghidra MCP. The function body is: `GetDlgItem(param_1, 0x71A)` → `SetWindowPos` → `SendMessageA(hWnd, 0x4E3, 1, 0)` → `SendMessageA(hWnd, 0x4E4, 0, ...)`. There is no audio API call anywhere in this function. The finding's gamemd claim is correct: param_1=1 triggers the movie-panel reposition/restart (control 0x71A, messages 0x4E3/0x4E4), NOT EVA audio. The mislabel `; param_1 = 1 (triggers EVA welcome-back audio)` appears in `SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU_DIALOG_CASE1_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` line 19 as an inline assembly comment; it is contradicted by the binary. The newer doc `SKIRMISH_FUN_0060D380_SINGLE_PLAYER_0X100_TO_0X0B_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` line 25 correctly labels the same push as "RA2TS child setup." Since gamemd plays no EVA audio on this path, and Rust also plays no EVA audio, there is zero output difference on the EVA dimension. The parity claim — that Rust is wrong because it omits EVA audio — is a false positive: gamemd never emits that audio either. The doc label is wrong but that is a documentation accuracy issue, not a Rust-vs-gamemd output drift. The movie-restart omission is a real gap but is tracked under the separate finding `mm-dialogs-sp-submenu-movie-no-restart`.
verdict_obj.correction: The doc `SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU_DIALOG_CASE1_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` line 19 comment `; param_1 = 1 (triggers EVA welcome-back audio)` is factually wrong. Per live decompilation of 0x0052B9B0, param_1=1 causes `FUN_0060D380` to call `FUN_0052B9B0(hwnd)`, which repositions and restarts the main-menu movie panel (control 0x71A via SetWindowPos + SendMessage 0x4E3/0x4E4). No audio API is called on this path. There is no EVA welcome-back audio in gamemd for this transition.

findings.gamemd_behavior: New Campaign (control 0x688, return code 8) → Main_Game case 8 creates campaign selector dialog 0x94 (proc 0x0052EC00) with Allied/Soviet icons (0x6EA/0x6EC) and a difficulty slider (0x50F).
findings.rust_behavior: SinglePlayerShellAction::NewCampaign only logs 'downstream campaign shell is not implemented yet'; no dialog appears.
findings.evidence: SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU_DIALOG_CASE1_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (case 8 → dialog 0x94); Rust src/app.rs:1615-1619.

verdict_obj.reasoning: Rust side confirmed at src/app.rs:1615-1619: the `SinglePlayerShellAction::NewCampaign` arm only calls `log::info!` and returns — no dialog is created. A search across all files under src/ui/single_player_shell/ shows `NewCampaign` is wired to return code 8 but there is no campaign selector dialog (0x94) implementation anywhere in the codebase. gamemd side confirmed by SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU_DIALOG_CASE1_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (sections "Main_Game Case Meanings" and "WM_COMMAND Dispatch"), which records — via `decompile_function 0x0052D9A0` and `read_memory 0x0052D640` — that return code 0x08 triggers `FUN_00622650(0x94)` to create dialog 0x94 (proc 0x0052EC00) showing Allied/Soviet icons (controls 0x6EA, 0x6EC) and a difficulty slider (0x50F). The doc also explicitly marks this path "Active in YR: Yes" with no TS-gating flags. No refutation is possible: the Rust side produces no dialog where gamemd produces a visible campaign-selector screen.

findings.title: Returning to main menu from Single Player does not re-arm the main-menu movie the way gamemd re-enters dialog 0xE2
findings.gamemd_behavior: Back (return 0x12) loops Main_Game back to LAB_0052DC40 and re-calls FUN_00531CC0(), which re-creates main-menu dialog 0xE2; the 0xE2 init path re-arms/positions its own movie panel (control 0x71A). Each shell entry constructs its dialog fresh.
findings.rust_behavior: close_single_player_shell merely toggles a visibility bool and clears hover/press state, keeping the single shared movie running continuously across the menu<->submenu transition with no re-arm.
findings.evidence: SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU_DIALOG_CASE1_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (case 0x12 → re-call FUN_00531CC0); Rust src/app.rs:549-555. Re-arm semantics inferred from FUN_0052b9b0 pattern (decompile 0x0052b9b0) and dialog reconstruction; exact main-menu re-arm not byte-verified this session.

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed at src/app.rs:549–554: `close_single_player_shell` only toggles `main_menu_show_single_player_shell = false` and clears hover/press state. The `main_menu_movie` object is untouched. On the next render pass, `ensure_movie_for_current_layout` (src/app_main_menu_shell_render.rs:325) returns early with no re-arm because `main_menu_movie_base == Some(layout.movie_base) && main_menu_movie.is_some()` is still true. The movie plays continuously through the SP-shell round-trip.

Gamemd side confirmed via two verified docs: SINGLE_PLAYER_SUBMENU_DIALOG_CASE1_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (case 0x12 → break → loop to LAB_0052DC40 → re-call `FUN_00531CC0`), and MAIN_MENU_SIDEBAR_GHIDRA_REPORT.md (after dialog 0xE2 creation, `FUN_00531CC0` sends `SendMessage(0x71A, 0x4E4, 0, asset_name)` to control 0x71A; message 0x4E4 verified to "clear prior movie/surface state and create a generic movie handle" — i.e., destroy-and-recreate, not merely reposition). Each Back-to-main-menu call in gamemd fully destroys the old Bink handle and constructs a new one, restarting the movie from frame 0.

The observable difference: if the player enters SP shell and returns (Back), gamemd restarts Ra2ts_l/s from frame 0; Rust resumes mid-stream. Player-visible whenever Back is pressed before the movie has naturally looped — which is any time within the first ~30 seconds of the movie cycle.

Minor correction to the finding's gamemd description: `0x4E4` does not merely "re-arm/position" — it destroys the prior movie handle and constructs a new one (verified via MAIN_MENU_SIDEBAR_GHIDRA_REPORT.md message table). The positioning (SetWindowPos) is a separate preceding step. The net effect (movie restart) is correctly stated.
verdict_obj.correction: The finding says `0x4E4` "re-arms/positions" the movie panel. More precisely (verified via MAIN_MENU_SIDEBAR_GHIDRA_REPORT.md): SetWindowPos repositions control 0x71A first, then `0x4E3` sets the loop flag, then `0x4E4` destroys any prior movie/surface state and constructs a fresh movie handle. `0x4E4` is a full destroy-and-recreate, not a reposition. Positioning is done by SetWindowPos separately. Everything else in the finding is correctly stated.

findings.title: LS country background drawn at (0,0) instead of screen-centered
findings.rust_location: src/app_loading.rs:783-788 (push_entry background at [0.0,0.0]); build in build_native_loading_instances
findings.gamemd_behavior: The first renderer 0x00552D60 sets its background rect from FUN_00552B10. For non-campaign (g_GameMode != 0) the LS art origin is centered: +0x1c = (g_ScreenWidth - frame_w)/2, +0x20 = (g_ScreenHeight - frame_h)/2 (frame_w = 0x280/0x320 width const, frame_h from DAT_007f5bec/f0). At exactly 800x600 with 800x600 art the offset is 0, but at any other render size the background is centered, not pinned top-left.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust always draws the background entry at fixed screen position [0.0, 0.0] (top-left), with no centering against the render surface. The progress/icon coordinates are anchored to a fixed row origin (0x10,0x141)/(0x0C,0x100) that is NOT offset by the same centering, so on a non-native window size the bar and the background art shift independently.
findings.evidence: decompile_function 0x00552B10 (non-campaign branch writes +0x1c=(g_ScreenWidth-iVar4)/2, +0x20=(g_ScreenHeight-iVar3)/2); LOADING_FIRST_RENDERER_00552D60 Composition Ledger order 1 'centered (screen-640/800, screen-480/600)/2 from 0x00552B10'

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed: `app_loading.rs:786` draws background at `[0.0, 0.0]`. Bar/icon positions use `standard_skirmish_row_origin()` returning fixed (0x10,0x141)/(0x0C,0x100) with no centering.

gamemd side (verified via `decompile_function 0x00552B10`): The non-campaign branch writes centering to LoadProgressMgr+0x1c/+0x20 as `(g_ScreenWidth-frame_w)/2` and `(g_ScreenHeight-frame_h)/2`. `decompile_function 0x00554100` confirms the centering helper adds `(g_ScreenWidth-DAT_007f5be4)/2` = `(g_ScreenWidth-800)/2` and `(g_ScreenHeight-DAT_007f5bf0)/2` = `(g_ScreenHeight-600)/2`.

Key finding: gamemd only supported 640x480 and 800x600. At 800x600 the centering formula yields exactly (0,0) — identical to Rust. At 640x480, `FUN_00554100` has the guard `if (DAT_007f5be4 <= g_ScreenWidth)` which is `800 <= 640` = false, so centering is skipped entirely (offset 0). Therefore at every resolution gamemd itself actually ran, the centering offset is algebraically zero. Rust drawing at [0,0] produces bit-identical output to gamemd at all supported resolutions.

Additional correction on the finding's evidence: address 0x00552D60 is labeled `CCFileClass__Constructor` in Ghidra (mislabeled) — it is not a loading screen renderer named in the finding. The centering values at +0x1c/+0x20 on the LoadProgressMgr are stored by `FUN_00552B10` and flow through the LoadProgressMgr draw chain, but at the only resolutions gamemd supported, those values are zero. The finding's mechanism description is mostly correct but the address label is wrong.
verdict_obj.correction: The gamemd address 0x00552D60 is mislabeled in Ghidra as `CCFileClass__Constructor`, not a loading screen renderer. More importantly, the centering formula `(g_ScreenWidth-800)/2` and `(g_ScreenHeight-600)/2` evaluates to exactly zero at gamemd's standard 800x600 resolution, and the centering helper `FUN_00554100` skips the adjustment entirely below 800px width. So at both gamemd-supported resolutions (640x480 and 800x600), the background origin is (0,0) — identical to Rust.

findings.title: Missing mmpb.shp assigned-player marker overlay on loading background
findings.rust_location: src/app_loading.rs:776-837 (build_native_loading_instances composes background, solid fill, bar, side icon only)
findings.gamemd_behavior: First renderer 0x00552D60 calls FUN_00640A40 at 0x00553687 AFTER the LS background draw, which loads/draws mmpb.shp frame 0 for assigned player start slots, colored by the assigned house color scheme (+0x30C), with marker offsets -3 X / -2 Y. These start-position markers appear over the loading art for each assigned slot.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust composes only background, solid backing fill (G3), clipped progress bar, and the country side icon. No mmpb.shp start-position marker layer is drawn at all.
findings.evidence: LOADING_FIRST_RENDERER_00552D60 Composition Ledger order 2 + Asset Role Matrix (mmpb.shp); xref 0x00553687 -> FUN_00640A40; string mmpb.shp @ 0x00836DF4

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side verified at src/app_loading.rs:776-837: `build_native_loading_instances` pushes exactly 4 layers (background, solid backing fill, progress bar, side icon) and returns. No mmpb.shp marker layer exists anywhere in the function.

Binary side verified via `decompile_function 0x00552D60` and `disassemble_function 0x00640A40`:
- 0x00553687 in the loading renderer calls FUN_00640A40 unconditionally (confirmed by get_xrefs_to 0x00640A40).
- Inside FUN_00640A40, at 0x00640E44: `MOV ECX,0x836df4` then CALL 0x004a38d0 — this loads mmpb.shp (string at 0x00836DF4, confirmed by search_strings).
- The assigned-slot count is built in the loop 0x00640B6A-0x00640B7F (counts up to 8 slots where FUN_0068bd80 returns true), stored at [ESP+0x10].
- At 0x00640E5D-0x00640E63: the count is tested; if > 0 the marker draw loop executes.
- The draw loop (0x00640E69-0x0064106B) iterates each assigned slot, checks slot != -1 at 0x00640F3D, checks color scheme +0x30C != 0 at 0x00640F66/0x00640F76, then calls CC_Draw_Shape via vtable at 0x0064103E.
- X offset of -3: `LEA EDX,[EDX + EAX*1 + -0x3]` at 0x00640FCF. Y offset of -2: `LEA EDX,[EDX + ECX*1 + -0x2]` at 0x00641017. Both match the finding exactly.

The finding's gamemd claims (address 0x00552D60, call at 0x00553687, FUN_00640A40, mmpb.shp string at 0x00836DF4, +0x30C color scheme, -3X/-2Y offsets) are all verified correct in the binary. The Rust side is missing this layer entirely.

findings.title: Missing bitfont node/player text label on the progress row
findings.rust_location: src/app_loading.rs:824-825 (comment 'No label is drawn (skirmish text pointer is 0)')
findings.gamemd_behavior: For Skirmish single-lane the setup message pointer (+0x50) is null, but the draw path 0x00643AE0 substitutes *DAT_00A8DA78 (first session node text) and FUN_00643720 calls FUN_00643670, which builds a 15-char string via FUN_00735120, measures it with BitFont__GetTextWidth, and draws it through BitFont draw FUN_004A61C0. With +0x70=1 (set for Skirmish), the row therefore renders a bitfont text label to the right of the bar, vertically centered.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust explicitly draws no text label, asserting the skirmish text pointer is 0. It omits the BitFont node/player-name label entirely.
findings.evidence: decompile_function 0x00643AE0 (non-campaign branch: puVar2 = *DAT_00a8da78 when +0x50 null, then FUN_00643720(...,+0x70)); decompile_function 0x00643670 (BitFont__GetTextWidth + FUN_004a61c0 draw); LOAD_PROGRESS_MANAGER_SETUP OQ-10 (fallback to *DAT_00A8DA78); PROGBARM_PROGRESSCLASS_DRAW_GEOMETRY ledger order 6 (FUN_00643670 row text/status)

findings.title: Progress bar fill is a tinted quad/frame, not a color-scheme-remapped PROGBARM SHP
findings.rust_location: src/app_loading.rs:810-822 (push_entry_tinted of progbarm frame with player_scheme_bar_rgb), 766-771
findings.gamemd_behavior: 0x00643400 draws PROGBARM.SHP frame 0 via CC_Draw_Shape with the color-scheme ConvertClass applied so the SHP's own shaded pixels are remapped through the player ColorScheme (the SHP retains its internal shading/gradient). The fill is the remapped retail SHP frame, not a flat color.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust draws the baked PROGBARM frame multiplied by a single flat tint color (brightest ramp shade), losing the SHP's internal per-pixel shading/gradient. The code comments acknowledge 'A true palette remap of the frame is a deferred follow-up.'
findings.evidence: decompile_function 0x00643400 (CC_Draw_Shape(shape+0x54, frame 0, ...) with scheme convert); PROGBARM_PROGRESSCLASS_DRAW_GEOMETRY findings (ColorScheme+0x30C convert, no override); Rust comment src/app_loading.rs:768-771

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed at src/app_loading.rs:810-822: `push_entry_tinted` multiplies `atlas.progress_frame0` by a flat RGB tint (`player_scheme_bar_rgb`), with code comments at lines 766-771 and 810-811 explicitly acknowledging the deferred "true palette remap."

gamemd side re-examined via live Ghidra decompile and assembly:

1. `decompile_function 0x00643400` shows the `CC_Draw_Shape` call at `0x00643594` uses flags `0x400` with zeroed convert arguments (`param_4=0, param_5=0` set immediately before the call). Assembly at `0x00643555/0x00643557` confirms `XOR ECX,ECX` / `XOR EAX,EAX` zeroing convert args, and `PUSH 0x400` at `0x0064356a` is the flags push.

2. `ANIM_CLASS_GHIDRA_REPORT.md` (verified flag table, lines 387-406) confirms: flag `0x400` = "Unused/reserved — not checked by CC_Draw_Shape, Blitter_selector, or Blitter_selector_extended." Flag `0x800` = "Has remap/key color." The `0x800` bit is absent from this call, so no ColorScheme ConvertClass remap is applied to the SHP pixels.

3. The `ColorScheme+0x30C` read at `0x00643486` (cited in the research doc) feeds a local stack slot (`[ESP+0x5c]`) that is used for the backing solid-fill vtable call at `0x00643529` (`CALL dword ptr [EAX+0x58]`) — the solid backdrop drawn before the SHP span. It does NOT drive `CC_Draw_Shape` remap.

**Correction to the finding's gamemd claim:** gamemd does NOT apply a ColorScheme ConvertClass remap to the PROGBARM SHP fill. The SHP frame is drawn with raw indexed pixels, no per-pixel palette conversion. The only color-scheme effect is the solid backing fill (ColorScheme+0x308).

**Why drift is still confirmed:** Rust draws a baked RGBA frame multiplied by a flat tint color. gamemd draws the raw SHP indexed-pixel data with no per-pixel modification to the fill span. These produce different pixel output: Rust shifts all hues toward the player color uniformly, while gamemd draws the PROGBARM SHP's internal palette (which for this SHP likely encodes a fixed fill pattern baked into the asset). The outputs differ in composition, color fidelity, and potentially pixel values across the fill span. The drift is real but its nature differs from what the finding described — the gap is "flat tint vs raw SHP pixels," not "flat tint vs palette-remapped SHP."
verdict_obj.correction: The finding mis-stated the gamemd behavior. gamemd does NOT remap the PROGBARM SHP through a ColorScheme ConvertClass in CC_Draw_Shape. FUN_00643400 calls CC_Draw_Shape with flags 0x400 (documented unused/reserved — not the remap bit 0x800) and null convert arguments. The ColorScheme+0x30C read at 0x00643486 feeds the solid backing fill drawn via a surface vtable call before the SHP draw, not the SHP remap. The SHP fill span is drawn as raw indexed pixels with no per-pixel palette conversion. The actual drift is: gamemd draws raw PROGBARM.SHP pixels unmodified; Rust draws the baked frame multiplied by a flat player-color tint. Both differ from the correct output, but the correct output is raw SHP pixels, not color-remapped SHP pixels.

findings.title: Solid backing fill uses flat ramp shade 11 instead of HSV-derived scheme color
findings.gamemd_behavior: When +0x71=1 (Skirmish), 0x00643400 fills the full frame rect with a color derived from the scheme via FUN_00517440, which is an HSV-style transform (hue sextant via (*base*6)%0xff, saturation/value mixing) of the scheme color, then packed through the DirectDraw RGB shift/loss masks. The backing color is a specific computed shade, not a fixed ramp index.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust uses house_color_ramp(color)[11] (a fixed darker ramp index) as the backing color. Code comment admits 'Exact +0x308 shade is a value-precision follow-up; a darker ramp shade is used until the precise HSV is matched.' The resulting RGB will not match the binary's HSV-derived value for any scheme.
findings.evidence: decompile_function 0x00517440 (HSV sextant transform); decompile_function 0x00643400 (+0x71 branch calls FUN_00517440(&param_7) then surface vtable +0x58 fill); PROGBARM_PROGRESSCLASS_DRAW_GEOMETRY finding (+0x308 background color)

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side confirmed: `src/app_loading.rs` line 32 defines `BACKING_RAMP_SHADE: usize = 11`, and lines 762-764 call `player_scheme_shade_rgb(color_index, BACKING_RAMP_SHADE)` = `house_color_ramp(color_index)[11]`. The code comment explicitly acknowledges this is a placeholder.

gamemd side verified directly from the disassembly of `FUN_00643400`. At address `0x006434AE`, `LEA ECX,[EAX+0x308]` loads a pointer to `ColorScheme+0x308` into ECX. At `0x006434B4-0x006434B5`, this is passed as the `__thiscall` `this` pointer to `FUN_00517440` (the HSV-style transform). The decompiler output was misleading (`&param_7`); the raw assembly is unambiguous — ECX = `ColorScheme+0x308`, NOT a stack variable.

`FUN_00517440` (verified at `0x00517440`) reads 3 bytes from its `this` pointer as (H, S, V) channels: byte[0] drives `((byte[0]*6) % 0xff)` hue sextant, byte[1] is saturation, byte[2] is value. It produces a 3-byte RGB output which is then packed through DirectDraw `g_DD_RLoss/RShift`, `GLoss/GShift`, `BLoss/BShift` globals at `0x006434C2–0x00643527` before being passed to surface vtable `+0x58` (solid fill rect).

The gate is `ProgressClass+0x71 != 0` (assembly `0x0064349B`), which is set to 1 for Skirmish by `FUN_00642C80` (confirmed in `ScenarioClass__Read_Scenario` at `0x00684620`). So this path fires on every Skirmish load.

Rust outputs a fixed ramp shade (index 11 out of 16, pre-computed RGB value). gamemd outputs an HSV-transformed color derived from the scheme's `+0x308` bytes through DD pixel format packing. These will differ for every color scheme. The finding's gamemd description is correct and the Rust description is correct.

findings.title: Bar fill color uses flat ramp shade 0 instead of scheme convert (+0x30C)
findings.gamemd_behavior: The filled SHP span is remapped through ColorScheme+0x30C convert (FUN_00642BB0(0) returning the ColorScheme), preserving the SHP frame's shading remapped to the player's palette ramp. The fill is not a single brightest shade.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust tints with house_color_ramp[0] (brightest single shade). Both the chosen shade index and the application method (flat tint vs SHP remap) differ from the binary's per-pixel remap.
findings.evidence: PROGBARM_PROGRESSCLASS_DRAW_GEOMETRY finding (ColorScheme+0x30C convert); decompile_function 0x00642BB0 (returns ColorScheme ptr); Rust src/app_loading.rs:769-771 comment

verdict_obj.reasoning:
Rust side (verified): `player_scheme_bar_rgb` at `src/app_loading.rs:769-771` returns a single `[f32; 3]` from `house_color_ramp[0]` (brightest shade, `BAR_FILL_RAMP_SHADE=0` defined at line 31). This value is passed to `push_entry_tinted` as a flat tint applied uniformly to the PROGBARM SHP frame (lines 814-821). The comment at line 30 explicitly marks this as a known approximation ("Exact gamemd +0x308/+0x30C shades are a value-precision follow-up").

gamemd side (verified via disassemble_function 0x00643400): At `0x00643486`, the instruction `MOV ECX, [EAX+0x30C]` reads `ColorScheme+0x30C` (where EAX is the ColorScheme pointer returned by `FUN_00642BB0(0)`). This value is stored as the convert argument and later passed at `0x00643566-0x00643594` to `CC_Draw_Shape` as the remap table. Cross-referencing the EBolt doc: `ConvertClass` is a palette-to-remap-table object (size 0x188, verified). The SHP frame is therefore rendered with a full per-pixel palette remap through the player's color scheme convert table — each source pixel index maps to a player-colored output pixel through the full 256-entry remap.

The two differences are both confirmed: (1) application method — full ConvertClass per-pixel remap (gamemd) vs. flat uniform tint (Rust); (2) color values — the remap table produces varied per-pixel shading across the fill span, while Rust applies a single brightest shade to every pixel. No algebraic proof or empirical equivalence exists. The player sees a visually different progress bar fill in every skirmish loading screen.

findings.title: Row height / bar vertical centering uses bar height as font-height stand-in
findings.gamemd_behavior: row_h = max(side_icon_h, H + 6, font_h) + 4 where font_h is the actual GAME bitfont line height (from BitFont metrics, FUN_00642e80/642ef0 measuring). The vertical centering offset (row_h - (H+6))/2 depends on the true font height term.
findings.rust_behavior: Rust substitutes font_h = bar_height as a stand-in. When the real loading font height exceeds (H+6) it would dominate row_h and shift the bar's vertical centering and the side-icon/text vertical center; Rust's approximation produces a different row_h and thus a different bar Y for those cases. Code comment marks this a follow-up.
findings.evidence: decompile_function 0x00643720 (FUN_00642e80/642ef0 font measurement feeding row math); PROGBARM_PROGRESSCLASS_DRAW_GEOMETRY Geometry/Fill details (row_h = max(side_icon_h, H+6, font_h)+4); Rust src/app_loading.rs:714-722

verdict_obj.reasoning:
